replies.text:
@eyezj7412 If you are going to criticize that, then the critic is to the Superman backstory, because that was there from the start.

Jor-El always knew Kal-El would be a god to humans. In the Golden Age, Krypton had 10x Earth Gravity and he knew by Earth size it only had 1/10th of Krypton's gravity meaning he would be superhuman.
In the post-crisis he shown to know about the sun powering up Kryptonians to superhuman levels as well.

About the ship only carrying one baby it was because in the original version, Krypton was somewhat advanced but not crazy advanced and Jor-El only managed to build a small untested prototype for interstellar travel and banked his son's life on it because the other option was to stay on Krypton and die. 

DC later messed up everything with their power creep craziness by having krypton being a super advanced civilization with massive fleets of interstellar ships each capable of destroying planets, or other crazy tech like the Eradicator. In that setting, Jor-El not being able to scrap a ship for the 3 of them is simply absurd and terrible writing no matter what kind of religion of tradition you write there.

replies.text:
@programme9363 Oh, so now the Superman movie is “bad” because the allegory was “too obvious”? Be serious. Allegories always feel obvious once you’ve realized them — that doesn’t make them bad writing, it makes you late to the party. Darth Vader literally translates to “Dark Father,” but no one was whining about how unsubtle that was until after it became iconic.

Let’s be honest: you’re not mad because the message was obvious. You’re mad because you understood it perfectly — and it didn’t align with your backward, insecure worldview. You’re not critiquing the story; you’re reacting to the fact that it has a conscience, and you don’t.

What really grates on you is seeing Superman — a symbol of hope, unity, and compassion — reflect values you’ve spent your whole life mocking. You don’t hate the movie for being unsubtle. You hate it because it held up a mirror and you didn’t like what you saw.

So stop pretending you’re some master of nuance. You’re not fooling anyone. You’re just another petty reactionary, crying about “obvious” messaging because deep down, you know the message wasn’t wrong — it just wasn’t made for people like you.

author: @Gunslinger47
text: I’ve been a Superman fan since I was knee-high to a grasshopper. From the beginning, he wasn’t about raw strength — he was about restraint, hope, and standing for something greater than himself. Man of Steel said it best: “He will give the people of Earth an ideal to strive toward.” That wasn’t just screenwriting — it captured what Jerry Siegel himself believed when he created Superman. He once said, “I hoped Superman would be a hero whose deeds would inspire people.” That’s who Superman was — a moral compass. A light in the dark. But now, they’re tearing that legacy down and calling it progress. If this is what the future looks like… all I see is a world without heroes.

author: @tillaf6925
text: For me the problem is that the storytelling and plot were awful, unbelievable, and tone deaf. There is no tension, I never feel anxiety or any expectation of how it’s going to play out. Therefore, there is no emotional reward when things go well. Superman looks like Superman, but… does not act like Superman (he is emotional, out of control, and has to be saved all the time, and nobody seems to respect him either). Lois looks like Lois, but does not have Lois’s tenacity and bite when it comes to journalism or investigative reporting, also her dress choices are shit. The movie did JorEl and Lara so dirty, and Martha and Jonathan are clueless and also have to rely on the woman their son is dating to ask if he’s gonna be ok, wtf, like they do not know him or his powers (screaming into a phone like idiots so their son who can hear a pin drop on the other side of the world can hear them?). The dog was practically more of a main character than Superman himself, and frankly unnecessary. The justice gang was also unnecessary, bolted the movie and there was no point having the there, and really, turning on Superman in 5 seconds Gardner, WTF? A clean simple plot would have done better than this mess, the soundtrack is awful, the campy humor just, no. Overall this movie is a huge disappointment, and NO, I am not a Snyder verse fan, he also went overboard on a completely different direction. But if I had to choose, I chose the Snyder movies for all they are awful over this slop.
